round2_improvement_notes.linguistic: Agent acknowledged the lack of linguistic evidence and adopted the consensus from other agents, but did not add new linguistic analysis.
round2_improvement_notes.landscape: Agent synthesized cross-agent evidence (regulatory/meta) to reinforce its own landscape observations, but the conclusion remained unchanged.
round2_improvement_notes.botanics: Agent corroborated its botanical findings with infrastructure evidence from other agents, reinforcing the conclusion without changing it.
round2_improvement_notes.regulatory: Agent integrated landscape and botanic evidence to support its regulatory findings, maintaining the same conclusion.
round2_improvement_notes.meta: Agent used regulatory and botanic evidence to strengthen its meta-analysis of infrastructure, maintaining the same conclusion.

role_leakage_notes.landscape: Minor leakage: Referenced 'regulatory and meta evidence' (LHT, guardrails) to support landscape conclusion.
role_leakage_notes.botanics: Minor leakage: Referenced 'infrastructure evidence' (guardrails, LHT) from regulatory/meta to support botanical conclusion.
role_leakage_notes.regulatory: Minor leakage: Referenced 'landscape and botanic evidence' (Japanese Cedar) to support regulatory conclusion.
role_leakage_notes.meta: Minor leakage: Referenced 'left-hand traffic' and 'Cryptomeria' from regulatory/botanic to support meta conclusion.

verdict.judge_synthesis_notes: The judge correctly identified the convergence of evidence across all agents. It explicitly listed the key visual markers (LHT, white tubular guardrails, concrete poles, Cryptomeria) and correctly reasoned that the combination is diagnostic for Japan. The elimination of other LHT countries based on specific infrastructure styles was sound.
